GG.description: Very high likelihood of brown eyes (>95%)

AG.description: Intermediate pigmentation, likely green or hazel eyes (70-80%)

AA.description: Very high likelihood of blue eyes (>90%)

description: Primary determinant of eye color variation in Europeans. This SNP in the HERC2 gene regulates OCA2 expression, controlling melanin production in the iris.

CC.description: Normal melanin production, contributes to darker eye color

CT.description: Slightly reduced melanin, may lighten eye color

TT.description: Reduced melanin production, contributes to lighter eye colors

description: OCA2 gene variant that modifies eye color by affecting melanin production. Works in combination with rs12913832.

GG.description: Higher melanin, contributes to darker eye colors

TT.description: Lower melanin, contributes to lighter eye colors (especially green)

description: SLC24A4 gene variant associated with green eye color variation. Secondary modifier of eye pigmentation.

CC.description: Normal MC1R function, no red hair from this variant

CT.description: Carrier of red hair variant, may have subtle red tints or increased freckling

TT.description: High likelihood of red or strawberry blonde hair (80-90%)

description: Most common MC1R variant associated with red hair. Loss of function mutation in melanocortin receptor, shifting eumelanin to pheomelanin production.

CT.description: Carrier status, possible subtle red tones

TT.description: High likelihood of red or auburn hair (75-85%)

description: Another common MC1R red hair variant (R151C mutation). Often found in combination with other MC1R variants.

CC.description: Higher melanin production, darker hair color

CT.description: Intermediate melanin, light brown or dark blond hair

TT.description: Reduced melanin, increased likelihood of blond hair in Europeans

description: KITLG gene variant strongly associated with blond hair in European populations. Each T allele increases blond hair likelihood.

CC.description: Normal melanin production, darker hair and skin

TT.description: Reduced melanin, lighter hair (especially blond/red) and skin, more freckling

description: IRF4 transcription factor variant affecting melanocyte function. Associated with lighter hair, skin, and increased freckling.

TT.description: Typical European/African hair characteristics, thinner hair strands

CC.description: Very thick hair strands, very straight hair (common in East Asians)

description: EDAR gene variant (370A allele) prevalent in East Asian populations. Affects hair follicle development, leading to thicker, straighter hair shafts.

AA.description: Reduced melanin production, light skin pigmentation (European-type)

AG.description: Intermediate melanin levels, medium skin tone

GG.description: Normal melanin production, darker skin pigmentation

description: Major determinant of light skin in European and South Asian populations. The A allele (derived) reduces melanin ~30-40%.

GG.description: Reduced melanin, lighter skin and eyes (European-type)

CC.description: Normal melanin production, darker skin and eyes

description: SLC45A2 variant affecting melanin synthesis. The G allele is nearly fixed in Europeans and contributes to light skin and eyes.

GG.description: Normal MC1R function, lower freckling tendency

GT.description: Increased freckling tendency, especially with sun exposure

TT.description: Strong freckling tendency, many freckles likely

description: MC1R variant (D84E) strongly associated with freckling, even in non-redheads. Also associated with fair skin and sun sensitivity.

TT.description: Each T allele adds ~0.4cm to height on average

CC.description: Neutral or slightly reduced height contribution

description: HMGA2 gene variant, one of the strongest single-SNP associations with adult height. However, height is polygenic with 700+ variants.

AA.description: Minor positive contribution to height (~0.3cm per allele)

GG.description: Neutral or slightly reduced height contribution

description: GDF5 growth factor variant associated with height and osteoarthritis. Small effect size as height is highly polygenic.

CC.description: Can taste PTC/PROP bitter compounds strongly (supertaster possible)

CG.description: Can taste PTC/PROP bitter compounds (medium sensitivity)

GG.description: Cannot taste PTC/PROP bitter compounds or very weakly

description: TAS2R38 bitter taste receptor variant (PAV/AVI haplotype). Determines ability to taste phenylthiocarbamide (PTC) and PROP bitterness.

TT.description: Reduced bitter taste sensitivity (part of AVI haplotype)

CC.description: Enhanced bitter taste sensitivity (part of PAV haplotype)

description: Another TAS2R38 variant that combines with rs713598 to form PAV/AVI haplotypes determining bitter taste sensitivity.

TT.description: Enhanced bitter taste sensitivity (part of PAV haplotype)

CC.description: Reduced bitter taste sensitivity (part of AVI haplotype)

description: Third key TAS2R38 variant completing the PAV/AVI haplotype. Works with rs713598 and rs1726866.

CC.description: Lactase production stops after childhood, high likelihood of lactose intolerance (>90%)

CT.description: Lactase persistence, can digest lactose in adulthood

TT.description: Strong lactase persistence, very likely can digest lactose throughout life

description: MCM6 regulatory variant controlling LCT (lactase) gene expression. The -13910 C>T variant enables lactase persistence into adulthood in ~35% of world population, mostly Europeans and some African/Middle Eastern groups.

GG.description: Normal ALDH2 enzyme function, no flush reaction

GA.description: Reduced ALDH2 activity, facial flushing with alcohol, increased hangover, higher cancer risk

AA.description: Very low ALDH2 activity, severe facial flushing, nausea with small amounts of alcohol, strongly protective against alcoholism

description: ALDH2*2 variant (Glu504Lys) causing aldehyde dehydrogenase deficiency. Common in East Asians (~40% carriers). Leads to acetaldehyde accumulation and 'Asian flush' reaction.

CC.description: Wet, sticky earwax (European/African type), active apocrine glands, more body odor

TT.description: Dry, flaky earwax (East Asian type), reduced apocrine gland activity, less body odor

description: ABCC11 variant determining earwax type and apocrine gland secretion. TT genotype (dry earwax) is nearly fixed in East Asians (80-95%) but rare elsewhere.

GG.description: Slower dopamine breakdown (Val/Val), better memory/attention, more anxiety-prone, pain sensitive

GA.description: Intermediate dopamine metabolism (Val/Met), balanced cognitive/emotional profile

AA.description: Faster dopamine breakdown (Met/Met), better stress resilience, lower baseline cognition, less pain sensitive

description: COMT Val158Met variant affecting dopamine metabolism in prefrontal cortex. 'Warrior' (Met) vs 'Worrier' (Val) cognitive-emotional tradeoff.
Additional OCA2 region variant modifying eye color. Secondary modifier with smaller effect than rs12913832.

AA.description: Normal opioid receptor, higher baseline pain sensitivity, better response to opioid medications

AG.description: Intermediate pain sensitivity and opioid response

GG.description: Altered opioid receptor (Asn40Asp), reduced pain sensitivity, may need higher opioid doses

description: OPRM1 mu-opioid receptor variant (A118G) affecting pain perception and opioid medication response. G allele reduces receptor expression.

AA.description: Reduced oxytocin sensitivity, less empathetic behavior, more stress reactivity

AG.description: Intermediate oxytocin signaling and social behavior

GG.description: Enhanced oxytocin sensitivity, more empathetic, better social skills, lower stress

description: Oxytocin receptor variant associated with empathy, social behavior, and stress resilience. GG genotype linked to more prosocial behavior.

AA.description: Increased androgen receptor sensitivity, higher risk of male pattern baldness

AG.description: Intermediate risk (only in females, males have one copy on X chromosome)

GG.description: Reduced androgen receptor sensitivity, lower baldness risk

description: Androgen receptor gene variant on X chromosome. Males inherit from mother. Affects sensitivity to DHT, primary driver of male pattern baldness.

CC.description: Normal D2 receptor density, less impulsive, lower addiction risk

CT.description: Intermediate dopamine signaling and behavioral traits

TT.description: Reduced D2 receptor density (Taq1A), more novelty seeking, impulsivity, addiction susceptibility

description: ANKK1 variant (formerly called DRD2 Taq1A) affecting dopamine D2 receptor expression. T allele associated with ~30% reduction in receptor density.

GG.description: Normal MC1R function, typical sun sensitivity for skin type

GA.description: Partial MC1R dysfunction, burns more easily, higher melanoma risk

AA.description: Reduced MC1R function, very sun sensitive, high melanoma risk

description: MC1R variant (V92M) associated with sun sensitivity and melanoma risk, even in individuals without red hair.

TT.description: Each T allele adds ~0.1cm to head circumference on average

CC.description: Neutral or slightly reduced head circumference

description: HMGA2 variant associated with head circumference variation. Same gene region affects overall height. Small individual effect.

TT.description: Normal weight regulation, lower BMI tendency

AT.description: ~1.5kg higher weight on average, moderately increased obesity risk

AA.description: ~3kg higher weight on average, increased appetite, 1.7x obesity risk

description: FTO gene variant, strongest common genetic predictor of obesity. Each A allele increases weight ~1-1.5kg. Affects appetite regulation and energy expenditure.

AA.description: Rapid caffeine breakdown, can drink coffee late without sleep issues, may need more for effects

CC.description: Slow caffeine breakdown, caffeine effects last longer, may have sleep issues with afternoon coffee, increased hypertension risk with high intake

description: CYP1A2*1F variant determining caffeine metabolism speed. AA (fast) metabolizers clear caffeine ~4x faster than CC (slow). Affects coffee tolerance and cardiovascular effects.

CC.description: Normal cilantro taste - perceives herb flavor

description: Genetic variation affecting olfactory receptor determining whether cilantro tastes like herbs or soap.

GG.description: Can detect asparagus metabolite odor in urine

description: Olfactory receptor variant determining ability to smell asparagus metabolites in urine.

TT.description: Strong sneeze reflex when exposed to bright light

description: Autosomal dominant trait causing sneezing when suddenly exposed to bright light.

AG.description: Likely to have dimples on one or both cheeks

description: Facial muscle variation creating visible indentations in cheeks when smiling.
Variation in jaw bone fusion creating a visible Y-shaped dimple in the chin.
Characteristic V-shaped point in the center of the hairline.

AA.description: Cannot roll tongue into tube shape

GG.description: Can easily roll tongue into tube shape

description: Ability to curl the lateral edges of the tongue upward into a tube shape.
Circadian rhythm variation determining natural preference for morning or evening activity.
Adenosine deaminase variant affecting deep sleep duration and sleep quality.
COMT haplotype variant modulating catecholamine levels and pain perception thresholds.
Genetic predisposition to strong emotional reactions triggered by specific sounds like chewing.

TT.description: Enhanced sweet taste sensitivity - prefers less sugar

description: Sweet taste receptor variant affecting sensitivity to sugars and sweet taste preference.
Transcription factor variant influencing eyebrow hair growth pattern and density.

TT.description: Highly susceptible to ice cream headaches

description: Cold-sensing receptor variant affecting susceptibility to cold-stimulus headaches.
Genetic variation affecting vestibular-visual conflict processing and nausea response.
HLA-associated body odor compounds affecting attractiveness to mosquitoes.
Vasopressin receptor variant associated with musical aptitude and pitch recognition.
Proprotein convertase variant influencing left-right body asymmetry and handedness.
Klotho protein variant associated with respiratory muscle tone during sleep.
Forkhead transcription factor variant influencing eyebrow hair density and thickness.

CC.description: Lower vitamin D levels - may need supplementation

description: Vitamin D binding protein variant affecting circulating vitamin D levels.
Strongest common genetic risk factor for Type 2 diabetes affecting insulin secretion.
FOXO3 variant consistently associated with human longevity across multiple populations.
ATP-binding cassette transporter variant affecting apocrine sweat gland secretions.
Interferon regulatory factor variant influencing melanocyte-driven freckle formation.

TT.description: Likely missing one or more wisdom teeth

description: Transcription factor variant affecting third molar tooth development and agenesis.
Collagen type V variant affecting tendon flexibility and injury predisposition.
Taste receptor variant determining sensitivity to glutamate and savory flavors.
Nicotinic receptor variant affecting susceptibility to tobacco addiction.
Trichohyalin protein variant affecting hair fiber shape and curliness.
Fucosyltransferase variant determining secretor status and norovirus binding.

GG.description: Enhanced cortisol sensitivity - stronger stress response

description: Glucocorticoid receptor variant affecting hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis sensitivity.
Ectodysplasin receptor variant influencing earlobe attachment pattern.
NAD(P)H dehydrogenase variant affecting retinal sensitivity to bright light.
Astrocyte glutamate transporter variant affecting cortical excitability and migraine.
Circadian rhythm gene variant affecting seasonal mood and energy regulation.
Serotonin receptor variant associated with sleep bruxism and jaw clenching.
HLA region variant associated with rare absolute pitch ability in musicians.
Uncoupling protein variant affecting mitochondrial energy efficiency during prolonged exercise.

CT.description: Heterozygous Factor V Leiden - slightly increased clot risk

TT.description: Homozygous Factor V Leiden - significantly increased clot risk

description: Factor V Leiden mutation affecting blood clotting cascade and thrombosis risk.
Gene variant associated with personality trait dimensions in large GWAS studies.

AA.phenotype: Greater VO2 Max Improvement with Training
AA.description: Greater VO2 max improvement with training

description: PGC-1alpha variant affecting mitochondrial biogenesis and aerobic fitness response.

TC.phenotype: Carrier for HLA-DQ2 Celiac Risk Allele
TC.description: Carrier for HLA-DQ2 celiac risk allele

CC.description: Higher celiac disease and gluten sensitivity risk

description: HLA-DQ2 haplotype tag SNP strongly associated with celiac disease susceptibility.

AA.description: Stronger fear conditioning and slower extinction

description: Cannabinoid receptor variant affecting amygdala-based fear learning and anxiety.

CC.description: Slower sunburn recovery and higher UV sensitivity

description: Melanocortin receptor variant affecting melanin production and UV damage repair.
Aryl hydrocarbon receptor variant influencing caffeine reward pathway and consumption habits.
Histone deacetylase variant associated with androgenetic alopecia susceptibility.

TC.description: Faster alcohol to acetaldehyde conversion - mild flush

description: Alcohol dehydrogenase fast-metabolizer variant causing acetaldehyde accumulation.
Cyclic nucleotide-gated channel variant affecting rod photoreceptor sensitivity.
Elastin gene variant affecting skin elasticity and striae formation.
